map.noJournal: No journal entry for this ride.

about.title: About
about.goalText: Ride from Deadhorse (Alaska) to Ushuaia (Argentina) — roughly 30,000 km across two continents, from Arctic ice to Patagonian winds. A fully self-supported journey departing summer 2025, shaped by roads, mountain passes and encounters along the way.
about.vincentText: Vincent Morisetti is a Swiss cyclist with a passion for adventure and wide-open spaces. After years of riding in the Alps and dreaming of a grand journey, he planned everything to cross both Americas by bike — from the Arctic Circle to Tierra del Fuego. An athlete at heart and endlessly curious about the cultures he rides through, he documents the adventure on video and Instagram to share every stage with his community.
about.sponsorsText: This journey is made possible thanks to the support of our partners. Want to support the adventure? Get in touch.
about.guideIntro: This map tracks Vincent's journey from Deadhorse, Alaska to Ushuaia, Argentina in near real time. Here is what you can see and where the data comes from.
about.guideSectionMapRides: Completed rides appear as orange traces. Click a trace to see the distance, elevation gain, date and ride journal if Vincent wrote one.
about.guideSectionMapRoute: The planned route is shown as a dashed cyan line. It represents the intended itinerary from Deadhorse to Ushuaia.
about.guideSectionMapPhotos: Geotagged photos taken along the road appear as markers on the map. Click one to view the image.
about.guideSectionMapWeather: The weather layer shows current conditions along the planned route: temperature, wind, humidity and a short description. Data is refreshed regularly.
about.guideSectionMapCities: Towns, mountains, passes and lakes near the route are marked. Click any of them to read a Wikipedia summary.
about.guideSectionMapPosition: The pulsing cyan dot shows Vincent's estimated current position: the furthest point reached on the planned route across all his recorded rides.
about.guideSectionDataTitle: Where does the data come from?
about.guideSectionDataStrava: Rides are imported automatically from Strava, Vincent's activity tracking app. Synchronisation runs approximately every four hours.
about.guideSectionDataFlickr: Photos come from his Flickr account. Only photos with GPS coordinates appear on the map.
about.guideSectionDataYoutube: Videos are imported from his YouTube channel and refreshed every hour.
about.guideSectionDataWeather: Weather data comes from the OpenWeatherMap API.
about.guideSectionDataWiki: City and point-of-interest descriptions are fetched on demand from Wikipedia, in your language when available.
about.guideSectionFreshnessTitle: How up to date is the data?
about.guideSectionFreshnessText: Rides and photos update every four hours. Weather refreshes regularly. There may therefore be a short delay between what is happening on the ground and what is displayed here.
about.guideSectionSuggestText: Have an idea to improve the map or the site? Send us a message.
